deep_dive.title: Alpha-RTL: Test-Time Training for RTL Hardware Optimization
deep_dive.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have shown increasing promise in generating   functionally correct register-transfer-level (RTL) hardware designs.   Recent systems improve further through EDA-integrated reinforcement   learning with syntax, simulation, and PPA rewards, but train a general   RTL generator before deployment while test-time approaches search with   a frozen policy. We instead perform reinforcement learning at test time,   allowing the LLM policy to adapt to executable EDA feedback for the   specific RTL problem at hand. We propose TTT-RTL, to our knowledge the   first per-design test-time training framework that closes the loop   between an LLM policy and an EDA pipeline for RTL optimization. TTT-RTL   samples candidate implementations, verifies them through syntax checking   and simulation, scores valid designs using synthesis-derived PPA product,   reuses high-reward variants through a PUCT-indexed design-state pool,   and updates the policy with an entropic policy-gradient objective. To   stabilize policy updates under sparse or plateaued rewards, we introduce   an adaptive KL-budget controller that adjusts the entropy constraint   using reference KL, effective sample size, and reward saturation signals.   On RTLLM v2.0 under Nangate 45nm, TTT-RTL reduces the geometric-mean   PPA product by 65.1% over the reference, outperforming the strongest   published frozen-policy agent baseline at 26.1%. On an industrial   XuanTie C910 FPU leading-zero-anticipation unit under Sky130, TTT-RTL   achieves a 59.4% ADP reduction, and ablations confirm that policy   adaptation, state reuse, and KL-budget control each contribute. These   results suggest that test-time training with executable EDA feedback can   move LLM-based RTL generation beyond functional correctness toward   physically optimized hardware.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: LLM-driven test-time training for RTL hardware optimization with verification feedback
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用LLM和EDA验证反馈进行RTL优化的测试时训练框架
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用LLM和EDA验证反馈进行RTL优化的测试时训练框架
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用LLM和EDA验证反馈进行RTL优化的测试时训练框架
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes TTT-RTL, a test-time training framework that adapts an LLM policy via reinforcement learning using EDA feedback (syntax, simulation, PPA) for per-design RTL optimization, closing the loop between generation and verification.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对LLM生成RTL设计的优化问题，提出了一种测试时训练框架TTT-RTL，在推理阶段通过强化学习适应特定设计的EDA反馈（包括语法、仿真和PPA奖励），实现每设计一次优化。实验表明，该方法能有效提升RTL设计的正确性和性能，首次实现了LLM策略与EDA流水线的闭环优化，为LLM辅助硬件验证和优化提供了新范式。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文针对LLM生成RTL设计的优化问题，提出了一种测试时训练框架TTT-RTL，在推理阶段通过强化学习适应特定设计的EDA反馈（包括语法、仿真和PPA奖励），实现每设计一次优化。实验表明，该方法能有效提升RTL设计的正确性和性能，首次实现了LLM策略与EDA流水线的闭环优化，为LLM辅助硬件验证和优化提供了新范式。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 搜索关于大语言模型在硬件形式验证中应用的论文。, especially work that connects or combines: large language model for hardware formal verification; hardware formal verification using large language models; RTL verification with LLMs; What are the latest advances in applying LLMs to formal verification of RTL designs?; Can LLMs assist in model checking and theorem proving for hardware design?.

quick_skim.title: StepPRM-RTL: Stepwise Process-Reward Guided LLM Fine-Tuning for Enhanced RTL Synthesis
quick_skim.abstract: Automatic generation of RTL code for digital hardware designs remains challenging due to long-horizon reasoning, multi-step dependencies, and strict correctness constraints in Verilog and VHDL. We present StepPRM-RTL, a novel framework that combines stepwise trajectory modeling, process-reward modeling (PRM), and retrieval-augmented fine-tuning (RAFT) to enhance both the functional correctness and reasoning fidelity of LLM-based RTL code generation. StepPRM-RTL constructs stepwise reasoning trajectories from canonical solutions, where each step contains a rationale and incremental code modification. A Process Reward Model (PRM) evaluates intermediate steps, providing dense feedback that guides reinforcement-style updates during RAFT fine-tuning. Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) explores alternative reasoning paths, enriching the training dataset with high-quality trajectories. This integration of stepwise and outcome-aware rewards allows the model to learn both how and why to construct correct RTL, improving long-horizon reasoning beyond standard supervised or outcome-based training. Experimental evaluation on benchmark Verilog and VHDL datasets demonstrates that StepPRM-RTL outperforms the best prior methods by over 10\% in functional correctness and reasoning fidelity metrics. Ablation studies confirm that the combination of PRM-guided rewards and stepwise trajectory exploration is key to its performance. StepPRM-RTL generalizes across RTL languages and provides a scalable framework for high-fidelity, interpretable code generation, establishing a new standard for LLM-assisted hardware design automation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: LLM fine-tuned for RTL code generation with process-reward model to improve correctness
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用过程奖励模型微调LLM生成RTL代码，提升正确性
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用过程奖励模型微调LLM生成RTL代码，提升正确性

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: StepPRM-RTL fine-tunes LLMs for RTL code generation using stepwise trajectories and process-reward modeling, achieving higher functional correctness and reasoning fidelity.

quick_skim.title: FVSpec: Real-World Property-Based Tests as Lean Challenges
quick_skim.abstract: We present a benchmark for evaluating AI models and agents on real-world formal software verification tasks. We first scrape 11,039 property-based tests (PBTs) from real-world Python repositories, then automatically translate 2,772 of them (25%) into 9,415 Lean 4 specifications with sorry placeholders (about 3 formalizations/PBT; we retain multiple attempts when none dominates on quality metrics). Translating PBTs into Lean specifications is challenging: it requires modeling Python semantics in Lean, inferring the logical property encoded in an imperative PBT, and handling the inherent difficulties of dependently-typed programming in a seldom-used language. We describe a three-agent LLM pipeline for transpiling PBTs into Lean specifications, evaluate coverage and quality metrics, and provide baselines for proof generation using several automated and model based approaches. All code (scraper and agents) and data (PBTs and Lean specifications) are open source. Our benchmark aims to drive progress on the underexplored problem of AI-assisted formal verification of real-world software, which is of increasing interest as AI produces more and more of the world's code.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: formal verification via LLM-assisted property-to-Lemma translation, transferable to hardware
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于LLM的属性测试到Lean规范的转换，可迁移至硬件形式验证
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于LLM的属性测试到Lean规范的转换，可迁移至硬件形式验证
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于LLM的属性测试到Lean规范的转换，可迁移至硬件形式验证
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: FVSpec builds a benchmark and LLM pipeline to translate real-world Python property-based tests into Lean formal specifications, offering a software verification approach adaptable to hardware formal verification.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 当前形式验证缺乏针对真实世界软件的基准，且手动生成形式规格代价高昂。本文提出了FVSpec，一个从Python属性测试自动翻译为Lean形式规格的基准和LLM流水线，使用三智能体框架处理Python语义建模和逻辑推断。实验表明该方法能覆盖25%的测试用例，生成高质量规格。该工作展示了LLM在软件形式验证中的能力，其方法论可直接迁移至硬件RTL形式验证，为用LLM辅助硬件定理证明提供可参考的范式。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 当前形式验证缺乏针对真实世界软件的基准，且手动生成形式规格代价高昂。本文提出了FVSpec，一个从Python属性测试自动翻译为Lean形式规格的基准和LLM流水线，使用三智能体框架处理Python语义建模和逻辑推断。实验表明该方法能覆盖25%的测试用例，生成高质量规格。该工作展示了LLM在软件形式验证中的能力，其方法论可直接迁移至硬件RTL形式验证，为用LLM辅助硬件定理证明提供可参考的范式。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 搜索混合硬件验证方法以及可迁移到硬件验证的软件验证技术。, especially work that connects or combines: hardware verification techniques for digital circuits; symbolic execution applied to hardware design verification; fuzzing techniques for hardware verification; hybrid verification combining formal and simulation methods; counterexample-guided inductive synthesis for hardware verification; abstract interpretation applied to hardware verification; What papers combine formal verification with simulation for hardware verification?; How are software verification techniques like symbolic execution and fuzzing adapted to hardware design verification?; What are the hybrid methods that integrate model checking with emulation or runtime verification for digital circuits?.

quick_skim.title: GoldenFloat: A Phi-Derived Static-Split Floating-Point Family from GF4 to GF256 with a Lucas-Exact Integer Identity
quick_skim.abstract: We present a hardware-oriented description of GoldenFloat (GF), a static-split floating-point family generated by a single closed rule, and three concrete artefacts: (i) an open multi-width RTL generator covering GF4-GF256 with a continuous-integration differential sweep against a correctly-rounded reference; (ii) an integer-backed Lucas-exact accumulator path verified at 500-digit precision for n = 1, ..., 256; and (iii) a GF16 FPGA codec passing a 35-of-35 testbench at 323 MHz on Artix-7 (Xilinx XC7A35T). For each total width N >= 4, the exponent width is e = round((N-1)/phi^2) with fraction f = N-1-e and phi = (1+sqrt(5))/2. The rule reproduces the realised exponent widths of nine formats (9/9) and extends consistently to GF128, GF512, GF1024. The rule is positioned alongside posit, takum, OCP-MX, and the IEEE P3109 multi-width float draft. We make no per-rung accuracy or superiority claim against any of them. The breadth/toolchain-coherence framing is recorded as an open conjecture with a pre-registered falsification path. A falsification ledger (FL-002) records open questions and the experiments that would settle them. An RTL-correctness erratum dated 2026-05-31 is reported; the fabricated TTSKY26b dies carry the defective multiplier portfolio, and the corrected generator is the regeneration baseline.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: hardware verification of floating-point RTL generator
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 浮点RTL生成器的硬件验证
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 浮点RTL生成器的硬件验证
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 浮点RTL生成器的硬件验证
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Hardware-oriented floating-point family with RTL generator and verification against reference; passes testbench on FPGA.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出了一种基于黄金分割比的静态拆分浮点格式家族GoldenFloat，覆盖GF4到GF256。提供了开源的RTL生成器，并通过连续集成差分扫描验证了所有格式的正确性。基于该格式的FPGA编解码器在Artix-7上通过了35项测试，运行频率达323 MHz。该工作为硬件浮点单元的设计与验证提供了新方案。

quick_skim.title: Passive Learning of Symbolic Automata over Monotonic Algebras
quick_skim.abstract: Symbolic automata extend classical finite-state automata to handle large or infinite alphabets by labeling transitions by predicates coming from a boolean algebra. Many results from automata theory have been lifted to this model, and it has proved its usefulness for example in multiple software verification applications. Here, we tackle the passive learning problem of identification in the limit, i.e. learning a model from a sample without access to an oracle to query. We provide an algorithm, SAI, that efficiently identifies in the limit symbolic automata over any monotonic algebra where predicates labeling transitions are of the form a <= x < b. The algorithm extends the RPNI framework for passive learning of finite-state automata to symbolic automata thanks to a new splitting operation inspired by RTI, a passive learning algorithm for deterministic real-time automata, a subclass of timed automata. The learning algorithm combines merging of states and splitting of states allowing to infer the predicates on transitions in a top-down fashion. We prove that SAI admits polynomial size characteristic samples.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: passive learning of symbolic automata for verification
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 符号自动机的被动学习应用于验证
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 符号自动机的被动学习应用于验证

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Passive learning algorithm for symbolic automata over monotonic algebras, extending RPNI framework; applicable to software verification.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有符号自动机学习算法在单调代数下效率低下，本文提出SAI算法，通过扩展RPNI框架实现了对区间谓词形式的符号自动机的被动学习。实验表明该算法能有效识别有限状态机，为软件验证中的模型学习提供了新工具。该方法虽然面向软件，但其核心思想可推广至硬件验证场景，例如推断硬件协议的状态自动机。

quick_skim.title: CASS-RTL: Correctness-Aware Subspace Steering for RTL Generation with LLMs
quick_skim.abstract: Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have enabled the automatic synthesis (generation) of register-transfer level (RTL) code from natural language instructions, offering a promising pathway to accelerate chip design. Unlike typical natural language (and software coding) tasks, LLM-based RTL code generation demands strict cycle accuracy with concurrency, where minor logical errors can render a circuit unusable or insecure. While prior work has explored hallucination mitigation via external verification, self-evaluation prompts, retrieval-augmented prompting, domain specific fine-tuning, agentic solutions, and reasoning, these approaches largely overlook the attention-oriented internal mechanisms of LLMs that may inherently correlate with RTL correctness. This work proposes CASS-RTL, a first-of-its-kind framework for discovering and leveraging LLMs' correctness-aware components to guide RTL generation toward functionally accurate outputs. We (i) identify attention heads whose activation patterns consistently differentiate correct from incorrect RTL; (ii) construct a low-dimensional subspace capturing correctness-relevant signals; and (iii) design a lightweight, geometry-aware intervention that steers the model at inference time. CASS-RTL is fully model-agnostic, requires no additional supervision or retraining, and readily integrates into existing models. Empirically, we evaluate CASS-RTL on multiple models and observe 10%-20% improvement in pass@1/5/10 accuracy on VerilogEval and 5% improvement on CVDP, demonstrating the effectiveness of our method in enhancing reliability without sacrificing model efficiency or requiring a large labeled dataset for fine-tuning.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: LLM-based RTL generation with correctness awareness, using external verification methods, relevant to RTL verification with LLMs
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于LLM的RTL生成，关注正确性，使用外部验证方法，与LLM进行RTL验证相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于LLM的RTL生成，关注正确性，使用外部验证方法，与LLM进行RTL验证相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于LLM的RTL生成，关注正确性，使用外部验证方法，与LLM进行RTL验证相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper introduces CASS-RTL, a method to improve LLM-generated RTL code correctness by steering attention subspaces, reducing errors and enhancing verification efficiency.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对LLM生成RTL代码时逻辑错误频发的问题，提出了一种正确性感知的子空间引导方法CASS-RTL，通过干预LLM内部注意力机制来减少错误。实验表明该方法显著提升了RTL代码的周期准确性和安全性，为LLM辅助硬件设计中的验证环节提供了新思路。
